methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Southern Launch is a growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Lloyd Damp. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 13 comparable firms: 5 public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Two primary valuation methods were tested. Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-filter matches; while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making a reliable median unachievable. Public-comp anchoring, the second method, could not proceed because Southern Launch's revenue remains undisclosed—standard for private companies—preventing application of the Space & Aerospace sector P/S median of 64.2x across the 6 public tickers identified.

The absence of both usable comparables and disclosed financial metrics prevents a triangulated valuation range from being derived. Progression requires either: disclosure of Southern Launch's revenue and profitability metrics to enable P/S and earnings-based anchoring; identification of Southern Launch within a narrower subsegment (launch services, orbital infrastructure, etc.) where same-stage private comparables with disclosed valuations exist; or direct access to recent funding round documentation. Without one of these inputs, quantitative valuation is not supportable.
